behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Real failure can come from the speech-to-text model dropping/misassigning speaker labels; a stubbed transcript would hide that. This behavior depends on real AI output content (LLM-DEP), not just transport wiring. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on STT label preservation (single model-quality boundary), not multiple model decisions in sequence as the assertion target.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against real meeting audio through the real STT API and assert speaker labels in produced transcript map correctly to known attendees before summary attribution checks.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is parser/transform logic over transcript text to structured fields. The failure boundary is local extraction logic (MOCK-MISS says function-level), with no required real OS/network/model output. Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require module handoff failure to reproduce; a direct parser test can catch real parsing misses.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser fixtures with explicit assignment utterances and assert exact assignee/task tuples in the summary-input data structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model judgment about extracting/including assignments per attendee in generated summary output; deterministic stubs can always satisfy coverage and hide omissions (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: behavior can be validated with a real transcript-to-summary model call; text does not require validating multiple agentic stages end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript samples with known attendee-task ground truth, call the real summarization model, and assert per-attendee action-item coverage in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic routing/mapping once decisions are already identified. Real failures are schema-mapping bugs in local logic, reproducible without I/O or real models. Rejected Integration: no concrete cross-module state handoff is required by behavior text; routing can be validated at function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Provide input objects containing identified decisions and assert mapper places them only in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model output quality and placement of extracted decisions in generated summary. A fake model response would mask real misses in extraction/salience/section placement (LLM-DEP, MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow: transcript-to-summary model behavior is sufficient to expose this failure; multiple agentic stages are not explicitly required by this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts with known decision statements and verify those decisions appear in `decisions` section with acceptable recall, not only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires sequential processing across segments plus merged coherent output, involving repeated model-driven summarization and cross-segment consolidation (MULTI-STEP). Integration would miss model-step interaction risks; Agentic single-step is too low because correctness depends on multiple agentic steps chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Process a real >60-minute recording through production split/sequence/merge flow, then validate all segment outputs are incorporated and merged summary remains coherent and non-duplicative across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end pipeline across real upload/transcription/summarization/output delivery with multiple model-dependent stages and orchestration. Failures emerge from chained agentic steps plus infrastructure interactions; lower levels cannot represent this full boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run on a real meeting audio file in production-like environment and assert successful unattended completion with delivered structured summary payload.
